description: Query<TGeometry,TOut> hand-rolls a 5-field reader-effect record (Key, Requirement, RequiresContext, Ready, Evaluator) with private constructor + Build/Reject factories — the same shape Eff<RT,T> already provides via reader composition, with Requirement/Ready folded into the Eff itself.

Build / Build<TState> / Reject form a 3-overload constructor cluster — Build<TState> is just Build with a closure-captured state; one polymorphic Build with optional state via params or a builder record collapses three to one.
Reject is a 9-line factory invoked from every Unsupported<>() path; collapsing to a single static field plus a polymorphic Build that takes Fin<Unit> ready replaces both with one expression.

Five enum types (MassKind, CurvatureScalar, MeshCheckCount, ConformanceResidual, MeshFaceMetric, DeviationKind, GeometryKind, IntersectionKind, TopologyKind) carry a literal None=0 sentinel that is rejected on every entrypoint via guard switch — replace with non-nullable [SmartEnum<T>] subset OR drop None and require callers to pass a valid case at construction.

Bounds[Box|Oriented|Transformed|Center|Corners|Edges|Area|Volume] union has 8 cases driven by a switch in Bounds<TGeometry,TOut>(); Box/Oriented/Transformed dispatch to private generics with identical structure (typeof + match + Build). Could collapse via a Fold over a (Bounds case -> projection) dispatch table, eliminating 3 sibling private generics.

Measure[Length|Area|Volume|SpatialMidpoint|Centroid|Error|CentroidError|Radii|Principal] union spans 9 cases dispatched in Measure<>() then re-dispatched in MassMeasure<>() through a private MeasureCase enum — an internal enum mirroring the union shape. Collapse: drop MeasureCase, fold Measure case directly into the (mass × out-type) dispatch table.

Private enum MeasureCase {Error,Centroid,CentroidError,Radii,Principal} is a parallel discriminant of the Measure union and exists only to flatten Measure into MassMeasure's dispatch tuple — dead weight once the union case is matched directly.

MassMeasure has 15 nearly-identical case arms (3 measure-cases × 3 mass-kinds × ~5 type filters) calling LengthMass/AreaMass/VolumeMass with hardcoded operation names — a (kind,mass)->(name,project) dispatch table reduces this 19-line table-driven dispatch.

Three Principal overloads (LengthMassProperties, AreaMassProperties, VolumeMassProperties) bodies are byte-identical except for the receiver type; all three forward to OperationKey.Principal extension — collapse to one generic helper bounded by a structural shape or use Thinktecture-generated dispatch.
CurvatureProfile<TGeometry,TOut> wraps CurvatureProfileReady<TGeometry,TOut> for nothing more than a count<=0 guard; the guard belongs at the dispatch table seed inside CurvatureProfileReady.

Profile() reimplements Mean/Variance/Min/Max/Count statistics on Seq<double> with two separate folds — the project already ships Core.Runtime.FoldExtensions.StatsOf which produces a Stats record with Count/Min/Max/Mean/Variance/Rms in one pass. Replace.

ResidualProfileDistances duplicates exactly the Mean/Variance/Min/Max/Sum/SumSquares/Rms reduction from Profile() (Locate.cs:419), with a 6-tuple fold seed; collapse via the same Core.Runtime.FoldExtensions.StatsOf and a thin tolerance-check projection.

ResidualRmsDistances hand-folds count + sumSquares to compute Rms — Stats.Rms from FoldExtensions.StatsOf already returns this value; entire helper collapses to .StatsOf(...).Map(s => One(key, s.Rms)).
ResidualWithinToleranceDistances re-folds Maximum + Count + Valid; once StatsOf adoption lands, this becomes .StatsOf(...).Map(s => One(key, s.Maximum <= context.Absolute.Value)).

ResidualMaximum carries a 3-tuple (Count, Best, Valid) fold to find the sample with maximum Distance — Seq<ResidualSample>.MaxBy(s => s.Distance) on top of the existing TraverseFin/RequireValid validators is a one-liner.

ResidualDistances is a 7-line folded validation extracting sample.Distance with Location-validity check — expressible inline as samples.Traverse(s => Validate(s)).Map(xs => xs.Map(s => s.Distance)) using the existing TraverseFin extension.

Fractions(count, key) returns Seq<double> of evenly-spaced fractions in [0,1] — a one-call use of LanguageExt.Range or Enumerable.Range followed by a Map. Worth keeping as a reused primitive but the imperative-style fold inside is unnecessary; rewrite as Range(0, count).Map(i => i/(count-1.0)).

Samples(domain, count, key) is structurally identical to Fractions but maps each fraction through Interval.ParameterAt — these are one helper with different projection: Interval.Mid for count==1, then ParameterAt elsewhere. Collapse with a Func<double,double> projection.

CurveCurvatures and CurveScalarProfile are a 2-step pipeline that already exists as a Bind chain; CurveScalarProfile maps Vector3d.Length over CurveCurvatures' result — a one-line .Map(values=>values.Map(v=>v.Length)) call, no helper required.

ExtremeAlongDirection uses a manual Aggregate that compares dot products with a Choose helper to find the point with extremal projection along an axis — Rhino.Geometry.Curve.ExtremeParameters already returns the canonical extreme parameters; iterate them with MinBy/MaxBy on PointAt(t).Dot(direction).
Choose(current, candidate, isMax) is a 3-line ternary used only by ExtremeAlongDirection; once that helper folds to MinBy/MaxBy this method is dead.

Dot(point, direction) hand-rolls the dot product (point.X*dir.X + point.Y*dir.Y + point.Z*dir.Z) — Rhino.Geometry.Vector3d.Multiply(Vector3d, Vector3d) and Vector3d operator* both compute this; an explicit (Vector3d)point * direction is the canonical form.

ExtractCardinals computes the 6 axial extremes from a Curve via 6 ExtremeAlongDirection calls; the same logic appears as 'WorldCardinalPoints' (Locate.cs:14) and 'Quadrants' (Locate.cs:23) — three call sites, single helper, this part is fine — but it overlaps with the planar/non-planar fork that should be a Bounds.Corners projection on the curve's accurate bounding box.

QuadrantsFromGeom converts Polyline/Line/Circle/Arc to a Curve via owned-conversion just to call ExtractCardinals — a single Curve.As(geom) ecosystem helper or a polymorphic curve-coercion at the entry of WorldCardinalPoints/Quadrants makes WithOwnedCurve disappear.
WithOwnedCurve is a 4-line using-statement wrapper around ExtractCardinals — disposable management is a single using inside QuadrantsFromGeom or a Defer/Bracket from LanguageExt.
CurveFrame is a 12-line wrapper around CurveAt that just discriminates on perpendicular (FrameAt vs PerpendicularFrameAt); single-call-site for each branch — inline at the Locate dispatch table.

Two Divide<TGeometry> overloads (count and length) are structurally identical; one polymorphic helper accepting a (Curve→Point3d[]) project closure collapses both. Currently 2×11 = 22 LOC for one operation.

CurveAt<TGeometry,TOut> is a private builder used by 4 sites (Mid/CurveFrame/CurvatureAtCurve/DerivativeAt); it's a useful primitive but its internal switch on geometry/Curve duplicates the ApplyValidated logic in Analyze.Program.ApplyValidated — a Query<Curve,TOut> input projection would be cleaner.
ShortPath<TGeometry> is a single-call-site (Locate.cs:175) Eff builder; inline at the Locate dispatch arm rather than spelling out a generic for one consumer.
CurveAtLengthValue<TGeometry> is a single-call-site Eff helper from Locate.cs:103; six lines of generic indirection that inline cleanly.

FaceFrame<TGeometry,TOut> recreates DecomposeFaces+SelectFaces+Traverse pipeline already used by Faces<>() at Extract.cs:594 — only difference is the per-face projection (Brep -> Plane via FrameAtCentroid). The two should share a 'FaceFold<TFace,TOut>' primitive that takes a per-face projection closure.

Faces<>() and FaceFrame<>() each compose DecomposeFaces+SelectFaces+Many; only the per-face projection differs (Brep itself vs FrameAtCentroid). One polymorphic Faces<TGeometry,TFaceOut>(Faces aspect, Func<Brep,AnalysisRuntime,Fin<TFaceOut>> project) primitive eliminates the FaceFrame entrypoint.

RankByCentroidZ + SelectExtrema is a two-step (rank+filter) pipeline that exists only to support Faces.Top/Bottom; collapsable to a single .Traverse(...).Map(seq => seq.MaxesBy/MinesBy(z, tolerance)) using an extension that handles tolerance-equal extrema in one pass.

SelectExtrema(ranked, descending, tolerance) walks the ranked sequence twice (Aggregate to find extremum, Filter to gather equals-within-tolerance) — Seq has no native MaxesBy but FoldExtensions can host one; once added the helper is one expression.

FaceCentroidZ recomputes AreaMassProperties.Compute(brep,...) -> Centroid.Z — duplicates the project closure path used by AreaCentroid<Brep> (Measure.cs:517). The result is only the Z component; Bind to AreaCentroid then .Map(p => p.Z) reuses the existing canonical pipeline.

CurveEdgeMidpoint duplicates the validate→CurveAtNormalizedValue→One pipeline already centralized in Query.cs:383 CurveAtNormalized<>; CurveEdgeMidpoint is the same sequence with project=Curve.PointAt — replace body with CurveAtNormalized<Curve,Point3d>(curve, EdgeMidpointsKey, (c,t)=>c.PointAt(t)).

EdgeCurveMidpoints folds across IEnumerable<Curve> and is consumed by 3 callers (BrepEdgeMidpoints/SubDEdgeMidpoints/BoxEdgeMidpointsViaBrep) — multi-caller helper with stable semantics, KEEP. But the manual Aggregate is replaceable by Seq.Traverse over each curve through CurveAtNormalizedValue, dropping the explicit (Points,Context) tuple seed.
BoxEdgeMidpoints is a 4-line single-call wrapper that delegates to BoxEdgeMidpointsViaBrep — collapse to one method, the indirection adds nothing.
BoxEdgeMidpointsViaBrep is a 4-line wrapper that delegates to DisposeAndExtract — single-caller indirection.

DisposeAndExtract<Brep> is a 4-line single-caller using-statement wrapping EdgeCurveMidpoints; replaceable by `using Brep b = box.ToBrep(); return EdgeCurveMidpoints(b.DuplicateEdgeCurves(), context);` inline at the call site.

BrepVertices and BrepEdgeMidpoints share an identical 5-arm KindOfBrep guard (Sphere/Cylinder/Cone/Torus → fail with PrimitiveNoEdges/Vertices, default → DuplicateVertices/DuplicateEdgeCurves) — extract a single 'GuardAgainstPrimitive' helper or fold both into a polymorphic 'BrepLeaves<T>' that takes a (Brep→Seq<T>) projection plus rejection-key.

KindOfBrep is the single dispatcher of brep-shape classification across the partial; same Surface-shape tests are duplicated inline in Kind<>() (Extract.cs:251-258). Extract a SurfaceKind(Surface,context) primitive that returns GeometryKind.{Plane,Sphere,Cylinder,Cone,Torus,Surface}; KindOfBrep then becomes one branch that prepends 'Brep' prefix.

DecomposeFaces<TGeometry> uses a 5-arm switch (Brep/BrepFace/Surface/SubD/_) that mirrors the Vertices<> 7-arm and EdgeMidpoints<> 8-arm dispatchers — a generic 'Decompose<TIn,TOut>(geom, projections)' over a Map<Type,Func<...,Fin<Seq<TOut>>>> would unify all three.

Box<>() and ExtractBounds() (Measure.cs:537,555) cover the same input vocabulary (GeometryBase/Line/Polyline/BoundingBox/Box/Sphere) — Box is a Query<>.Build wrapper; ExtractBounds is the projection. The wrapper exists only to attach a key. Inline ExtractBounds inside the Query.Build evaluator.

ExtractBounds duplicates the BoundingBoxOf logic from Measure.cs:45 (UniqueCorners pipeline) — both extract a BoundingBox from a polymorphic geometry input. Two helpers, one concept; collapse to a single 'BoundingBoxOf' shared by Bounds.Box, Bounds.Center, Bounds.Corners, and BoundingCorners.

Vertices<>() (Extract.cs:311), EdgeMidpoints<>() (Extract.cs:54), and DecomposeFaces<>() (Extract.cs:611) form the same 'topological-projection' pattern: switch on TGeometry, dispatch to private per-Type extractor, validate, project. A single RepoQuery<TGeom,TResult> sealed DU + Fold dispatcher with per-Type projection closures replaces three near-identical entrypoints.

BoundingBoxOf<TGeometry> exists only to feed UniqueCorners/BoundingCorners; one polymorphic primitive should serve all bounds-extraction call sites (B-044 / B-045) — currently three competing 'extract a bbox' helpers across the partial.

DedupeCorners<>() walks bbox.GetCorners() and folds with Exists(p => p.DistanceTo(c) <= tolerance) — Rhino does not provide a tolerance-dedup primitive but Point3d.PointArrayBoundingBox or Point3dList.RemoveDuplicateNodes (Rhino 8) collapses point clouds with tolerance. Use the latter; manual O(n²) fold is wasteful and hand-rolled.

CurveSpatialMidpoint is a 5-line guard that picks AreaCentroid for closed-planar, LengthCentroid otherwise; the same heuristic belongs in the SpatialMidpoint dispatch arm (Measure.cs:154) as an inline ternary or pattern-arm.
SubDSpatialMidpoint and SubDBrepSpatialMidpoint are a 2-helper chain (validate → ToBrep → dispose-and-recurse) used only at one Measure.cs:164 dispatch arm; one inline expression replaces both.

SubDBrepSpatialMidpoint switches on Brep.IsSolid to pick Volume vs Area centroid — same guard appears at Measure.cs:160-163 for Mesh/Surface; consolidate the IsSolid-branching into a single 'CentroidByVolumeOrArea' primitive over GeometryBase.

MassCentroid<TGeometry> wraps Validate + query.Apply for any GeometryBase — its body is exactly what Analyze.Program.ApplyValidated already does; the helper exists because SpatialMidpoint dispatches at the geometry-shape level rather than letting the Query<>.Apply handle it. Remove once SpatialMidpoint is rewritten as a typed dispatch table.

Pair / PairEvents / PairCurvePoint / PairPolylines form a 4-overload cluster of identical structure (Pair builder + per-shape adapter); a single 'Pair<TA,TB,TLeft,TRight,TOut>(key,a,b,outputProjection)' primitive plus inline closures at each Intersect arm collapses ~75 LOC of plumbing.

Events(output) and Curves(output) are two-line OR-chains used 11 times across Intersect<> dispatch — predicates that belong in the dispatch table itself or as static readonly Set<Type>; the helpers add an indirection layer for negligible savings.

CurveDeviationValue carries an 8-field validity check (deviation.MinimumA.IsValid && ... .IsValid) — Core.Runtime.RhinoMatch.RequireValid already validates Point3d; chain the deviation construction through ConformanceKey.RequireValid for each component instead of an 8-conjunct predicate.

ValidatePoints uses Enumerable.Range + Aggregate to traverse a span — Span<Point3d>.ToArray().Map(point.IsValid?...).TraverseFin via FoldExtensions is the canonical form once span-extension support lands; current form duplicates the TraverseFin pattern from FoldExtensions.cs:28.

ValidateBounds<TGeometry> duplicates ValidatePoints' Range+Aggregate template structure for BoundingBox accumulation; the two are the same 'TraverseFin over span' pattern — collapse to one polymorphic 'TraverseSpan<TIn,TOut>(span, project)' primitive.
Two Search overloads (BoundingBox + Sphere) at Spatial.cs:186,195 are byte-identical except for the RTree.Search shape parameter; one polymorphic Search<TShape>(tree, shape, dispatch) collapses both.
CollectHit and CollectPair are 2-line lambda factories returning EventHandler<RTreeEventArgs>; inline at the two callers (Search variants) — each is a single-call-site lambda.

Private nested SearchState class holds two parallel mutable buffers (ids[] + pairs[]) when a SearchState always uses one or the other (hits OR pairs, never both). Two small types or a discriminated union (SearchState.Hits / SearchState.Pairs) yields two single-purpose state holders.

LengthMass / AreaMass / VolumeMass at Query.cs:401,425,471 are three near-identical builders that differ only by (a) Mass-property class (LengthMass/Area/Volume), (b) compute-call signature, and (c) requirement constant. A single 'Mass<TGeometry,TMass,TOut>(...)' already exists at Query.cs:513; consolidate the three public-internal entries into one parameterized factory.

Mass<TGeometry,TMass,TOut> is the only implementation of the three public-internal builders (B-061); the wrapper exists for type variance, but a `where TMass : class, IDisposable, IMassProperties` constraint plus a generic compute lambda eliminates the trio. Currently the consolidator and three wrappers are 4 generics for one operation.
DisposeAndProject<TMass,TOut> is a 6-line single-caller helper for Mass<>(); the using-statement is a one-liner inline.

Mass<TMass>(this TMass? mass, string label) is a 3-line extension that wraps Optional + ToFin — used 4 times in Query.cs but functionally identical to inline `Optional(x).ToFin(MassFault.Failed(label))`. Worth retaining as a small named helper IF kept on the static class; otherwise inline.

Private nested MassFault class hosts two error factories (Failed, Unsupported) used only inside Query.cs Mass<>() pipelines; co-locate the strings with OperationFault in core/Domain/Operation.cs (where InvalidResult and Unsupported already live) — the parallel error-rail violates 'one Error subtype per concept'.

PrimitiveExtract<TSource,TValue> is a 13-line single-caller helper used only by PrimitiveMatch (Query.cs:320); inlines as `extract(geometry,context,out v) ? key.One(v) : Fin.Fail(key.InvalidResult())` plus the same Solved primitive already exposed via key.Solved (line 351).

IntersectionKinds(curves,points,polylines,intersections) is a 25-line builder that concats four optional sources into IntersectionKind values — used only by IntersectionOutput at Query.cs:273. Inline at the IntersectionOutput case arm; the helper exists for one consumer only.

Cast<TGeometry,TOut>(key, query) wraps a runtime-type test that downcasts a cross-type Query<,>; used at every public entrypoint (35+ sites) — useful primitive that should KEEP, but the 11-line body is replaceable by `query is Query<TGeom,TOut> q ? q : Reject(...)`.

Unsupported<TGeometry,TOut>() is a 6-line extension on OperationKey that is itself a thin shorthand for Reject(key, key.Unsupported(...)); the call-site readability gain is real (`key.Unsupported<TGeom,TOut>()` reads cleanly). KEEP — multi-caller domain operation.

One/Many extensions on OperationKey are 1-line forwarders to GeometryResult.One/Many in Core.Domain — two re-export wrappers that exist solely to add `key.One(value)` syntax over `GeometryResult.One(key, value)`. The convenience is real but the indirection violates 'no thin wrappers that rename external APIs'. Pick one form and use it consistently.

GeometryKind enum mixes raw geometry kinds (Curve/Polyline/Mesh/SubD/Surface), Brep specialization (BrepGeneral..BrepPlane), and primitive shapes (Line..Plane) into one enum with magic-number gaps (10..,20..). A discriminated union or three smaller enums is more honest about the discriminant axis.

Faces union has 4 cases (All/Top/Bottom/At) but is also dispatched from Faces<>() through an Index-Hint runtime channel; the 'At' case requires AnalysisRuntime.Index — that semantic coupling means Faces should really be a (Selector × Maybe<int>) record, not a union of 4 flat cases.

The Bounds union (Box/Oriented/Transformed/Center/Corners/Edges/Area/Volume) mixes shape-extraction (Box/Oriented/Transformed) with property-extraction (Center/Corners/Edges/Area/Volume); they are different domain operations. Split into Bounds.Shape vs Bounds.Property unions, or two top-level entrypoints.

Deviation readonly record struct has only one viable case (Curve) — the union is single-arm with a Kind enum. Either drop the union entirely (simply require a Conformance.Deviation case in the existing Conformance/Deviation primitive) or wait until Surface deviation is added; the empty-case structure is design noise.

Conformance readonly record struct has 5 static factory methods (Distance/Rms/WithinTolerance/Profile/Maximum) — could be a (ConformanceResidual, int Count) tuple; private constructor + 5 wrappers vs `new Conformance(residual, count)` is 12 lines vs 4.
Topology readonly record struct has 4 static factories for what is actually an enum-like 4-state discriminant; replace with the existing internal TopologyKind enum at Query.cs:123 used directly.

Program<TGeometry,TOut>.RuntimeOrSentinel synthesizes an uninitialized AnalysisRuntime via System.Runtime.CompilerServices.RuntimeHelpers.GetUninitializedObject when no runtime is supplied (BOUNDARY ADAPTER comment). This sentinel can leak into evaluators that assume `rt.Context.Absolute.Value` is callable; any mistakenly context-touching query crashes at AbsoluteTolerance.Value access. Replace with Option<AnalysisRuntime> and require evaluators to declare context-need at the type level.

Program<>.Apply / ApplyValidated / RuntimeOrSentinel form a 3-step pipeline that exists to bridge the synchronous Query.Apply contract to a runtime-or-sentinel reader; once boundary-leak (B-077) is fixed by typing context-need, the indirection collapses to a 2-line Bind.
Program<>.setup field combines query.Ready + (requiresContext switch) into a Fin<Unit>; this is a 7-line tuple-apply that's redundant once Query.Ready is folded into the Eff itself (B-001).

Program<>.Execute does a manual divide-and-conquer over the input span (length<=1 base case, otherwise split-and-recurse-and-combine) — for the typical Grasshopper case (1-32 inputs) this yields no parallelism benefit and makes the call stack opaque. Sequential `input.ToSeq().Traverse(Apply)` is simpler and equivalent for non-parallel batches.
Cast<TGeometry,TOut>(key, query) uses query.GetType().Equals(typeof(...)) — a more idiomatic + faster check is `query is Query<TGeometry,TOut> q`; same semantics, no Type allocation.

SpatialIndex.Overlaps allocates `state.Left.Count * state.Right.Count` capacity for SearchState.pairs — quadratic preallocation when overlap counts are typically O(n). Use a growable LanguageExt Lst<SpatialPair> seeded empty.

Kind<TGeometry,TOut>() inlines the same 5-arm Surface-shape test (TryGetPlane/Sphere/Cylinder/Cone/Torus) as KindOfBrep at Extract.cs:566; surface case in Kind<>() and KindOfBrep both implement the test — extract a single SurfaceShape(Surface,context) primitive returning GeometryKind.{Plane,Sphere,...}.

NakedEdges<>() and Topology<>(Boundary) at Extract.cs:382-383 dispatch to identical bodies (the Boundary case redirects to NakedEdges); collapse the 2-arm Topology.Boundary case-arm into the existing NakedEdges<TGeometry,TOut>() body.

Topology<>() Adjacency and NonManifold cases for Brep are nearly identical: both Range over brep.Edges.Count then Where + Select to ComponentIndex; only the Where predicate differs (true for Adjacency, Valence==NonManifold for NonManifold). One generic helper with a predicate closure replaces 2 dispatch arms.

Topology<>() Adjacency and NonManifold cases for Mesh mirror Brep's structure (same predicate split, same Range+Select pipeline) — symmetric with the Brep cluster (B-085). One generic dispatch over Brep|Mesh edge collections collapses 4 dispatch arms into 1.

Topology<>() NonManifold→bool case is the .Any() variant of NonManifold→ComponentIndex (same Range+Where, just .Any vs .Select+ComponentIndex); collapsable to one helper that returns either Seq<ComponentIndex> or its non-empty?-bool projection.

MeshCheckCount has 13 case-arms each forwarding `head.{XCount}` for a corresponding MeshCheckCount enum case; this is a 38-line dispatch table that maps Enum→property-getter — replace with a static readonly Dictionary<MeshCheckCount, Func<MeshCheckParameters,int>> + Map lookup, eliminating the switch.

MeshFaceMetric has 1 viable case (AspectRatio) yet uses a 22-line switch+aggregate body; the inner Aggregate-with-tuple-apply is the canonical TraverseFin pattern from FoldExtensions — replace with `samples.TraverseFin().Map(...)`.

SelfIntersections + SelfIntersectionsValue is a 2-step (Eff<>.Build + Fin computation); SelfIntersectionsValue contains a using-statement around TextLog plus a multi-out invoke — single-call helper, inline at the SelfIntersections evaluator.

MeshCheckParametersFor uses an imperative-looking using+ref pattern (using TextLog; mesh.Check(textLog, parameters: ref parameters)) — Rhino requires this for the ref-parameter, but the 'true or false => One(...)' switch arm always succeeds. Either propagate the textLog content into the error message or drop the switch; the boolean is dead.

key.Solved(solved, value) is the canonical bool→Fin<Seq<T>> bridge already exposed on OperationKey (Core.Domain.GeometryResult.Solved); CurveFrame's body inlines as `key.Solved(curve.PerpendicularFrameAt(t,out frame), frame)` cleanly without the local Plane variable shadowing.

4 private delegate types (ResidualSampleCase / ClosestPointCase / DistanceCase / ResidualProjection) are declared at class scope; ResidualSampleCase has 4 arity-positions, ClosestPointCase has 2, DistanceCase has 2 — Func<>/Func<,,> covers all four with named state types. Delegates add no nominal value.

Two private delegate types (PairOutput / CurvePointIntersection) at Intersect.cs:17,21; PairOutput is exactly Func<TLeft,TRight,GeometryContext,Fin<Seq<TOut>>>, CurvePointIntersection has out-parameters which Func<> cannot express. KEEP CurvePointIntersection (out-param justification); replace PairOutput with Func<>.

PairEvents wraps Pair with a using-statement+IntersectionOutput; consumes 13 lines for what is `(left,right,ctx) => { using var i = intersect(left,right,ctx); return key.IntersectionOutput<TOut>(intersections: i); }` — replaceable as inline pair-output closure when Pair is invoked.
PairCurvePoint is a 24-line wrapper that adds an acceptPartialResults branch + curves/points out parameters; eliminated once B-053 collapses the cluster.
PairPolylines is a 13-line wrapper that calls Pair with an inline polylines-output closure; once B-053 lands the entire helper is dead.

Bounds/Measure/Location/Faces/Topology/Conformance/Deviation/IntersectionKind are all aspect-discriminants for entrypoints (Bounds<>/Measure<>/Locate<>/Faces<>/Topology<>/Conformance<>/Deviation<>/Intersect<>). Each entrypoint dispatches its aspect through a switch with type-assignability guards — the same shape repeated 8 times. A single 'Aspect<TGeom,TOut,TAspect>(aspect, projection)' primitive driven by a per-aspect dispatch table collapses the structural duplication.

Reject() ignores the requirement parameter — it always passes GeometryRequirement.None; once Build() is consolidated (B-002) the constructor parameter list shrinks from 5 to 3 because Requirement and RequiresContext are derivable from the Eff structure itself.

CurvatureProfileReady<>() has 7 arms covering (Curve|Surface)×(None|Magnitude|Gaussian|Mean) and (Vector3d|Point3d|CurvatureProfile|double|SurfaceCurvature) outputs — 7 arms × 12-15 LOC each = ~95 LOC of dispatch noise. A single 'CurvatureSampling<TGeom,TOut>(scalar,count)' primitive driven by (Type,Type)→(sampler,projection) Map collapses to ~30 LOC.
